what_sql_is.label: What SQL Is
what_sql_is.summary: SQL is a language for working with organized data in databases.

sketch3.title: Where do databases appear in real life?

what_sql_means.title: SQL is a language for data.
what_sql_means.bodyMarkdown:
SQL is used to work with data in a database.

[[image:sql_language_overview]]

It stands for **Structured Query Language**.

The idea is simple:

> SQL helps you retrieve data, sort it, filter it, and sometimes change it.

[[image:sql_question_to_data_flow]]

If you had a table of students, SQL could help answer questions like:

- Who got an A?
- How many students are there?
- What are all the names?

[[image:students_table_example]]

A first example looks like this:

~~~sql
SELECT * FROM students;
~~~

Copy the code above, paste it into the SQL editor, and click **Run**. A table with student information will appear in the results pane.

[[image:select_from_breakdown]]

For now, just notice:

- `SELECT` = show data
- `FROM students` = from the students table

So SQL is a way to **talk to stored information**.

---

## Big idea

Many languages focus on steps.
SQL often focuses on the result:

> What data do you want?

[[image:sql_result_focus]]

what_a_database_is.title: A database stores organized information.
what_a_database_is.bodyMarkdown:
A **database** is an organized collection of data.

[[image:database_overview]]

Instead of scattered notes or random files, it keeps information structured so it can be searched, updated, and reused.

[[image:messy_vs_structured_data]]

A database might store:

- students and grades
- customers and orders
- products and prices
- employees and departments

[[image:database_real_examples]]

A simple way to picture a database is as a group of **tables**.

[[image:database_contains_tables]][[image:table_row_column_labels]]

In a table:

- a **row** is one record
- a **column** is one kind of information

[[image:row_vs_column_highlight]]

A useful summary is:

> a database holds tables

[[image:database_container_model]]

why_sql_is_useful.title: SQL helps turn data into answers.
why_sql_is_useful.bodyMarkdown:
SQL is useful because data is everywhere.

[[image:data_everywhere]]

Apps, stores, schools, and websites all save information. SQL helps you do something with that information.

With SQL, you can:

- read data
- filter data
- sort data
- summarize data
- add or change records

[[image:sql_actions_overview]]

That means SQL is useful for both building software and understanding information.

For example, SQL can help answer:

- Which products cost more than 20?
- How many students passed?
- What is the highest price?

[[image:sql_questions_and_answers]]

A common pattern is:

1. Ask a question
2. Run the query
3. See the result

[[image:ask_run_result_cycle]]

real_world_examples_of_databases.title: Databases appear in many everyday systems.
real_world_examples_of_databases.bodyMarkdown:
Databases are everywhere.

[[image:databases_in_daily_life]]

Any system that needs to remember information probably uses one.

### School system
A school may store:

- students
- teachers
- courses
- grades

[[image:school_database_example]]

### Online store
A store may store:

- products
- customers
- orders
- inventory

[[image:store_database_example]]

### Music or movie app
A media app may store:

- users
- songs or movies
- playlists
- favorites

[[image:media_database_example]]

### Hospital or clinic
A healthcare system may store:

- patients
- doctors
- appointments
- prescriptions

[[image:hospital_database_example]]

Even though these systems are different, the pattern is the same:

1. store information in tables
2. keep it organized
3. use SQL to ask questions about it

[[image:shared_database_pattern]]

m0_s01_sql_stands_for.title: What does SQL stand for?

help.concept: This checks the full name behind the abbreviation.
help.hint_1: One word refers to asking for data.

m0_s01_sql_stands_for.hint: Think of the full expanded name.

m0_s01_sql_main_purpose.title: What is SQL mainly used for?
m0_s01_sql_main_purpose.prompt: Which choice best describes what SQL is mainly used for?

help.concept: Focus on what SQL helps people do with stored information.
help.hint_2: Pick the option about asking for and managing data.

m0_s01_sql_main_purpose.hint: Think about working with stored data.

options.b: Asking questions about data and changing stored data

m0_s01_database_definition.title: What is a database?
m0_s01_database_definition.prompt: Which definition best matches a database?

help.concept: A database is about organized stored information.

m0_s01_database_vs_table.title: How is a database different from a table?

help.concept: This checks the relationship between the larger container and the pieces inside it.
help.hint_1: One is broader than the other.

m0_s01_database_vs_table.hint: One is broader than the other.

options.b: A database can contain multiple tables
options.c: A database and a table are always the same thing

m0_s01_table_shape.title: What does a row usually represent?
m0_s01_table_shape.prompt: In a table, what does a row usually represent?

help.concept: This checks the basic shape of tabular data.
help.hint_1: Think about one student in a students table.
help.hint_2: Look for one item or entry.

m0_s01_table_shape.hint: Think of one line in a table.

m0_s01_sql_command_recognition.title: What does SELECT do?
m0_s01_sql_command_recognition.prompt:
Look at this command:

~~~sql
SELECT * FROM students;
~~~

What does `SELECT` most directly mean here?

help.concept: This checks the role of a common SQL keyword.
help.hint_2: Choose the option about showing information.

m0_s01_why_sql_useful.title: What is one thing SQL can help you do?
m0_s01_why_sql_useful.prompt: Which of these is something SQL can help you do?

help.concept: This checks whether you connect SQL to practical data tasks.
help.hint_1: Think about actions on stored information.
help.hint_2: Pick the option about working with data.

m0_s01_why_sql_useful.hint: Think about actions on stored data.

m0_s01_real_world_examples.title: Which real system likely uses a database?
m0_s01_real_world_examples.prompt: Which option is the best example of a system that would likely use a database?

help.concept: This checks where organized stored data is needed in real life.
help.hint_1: Look for a system that must remember many records.
help.hint_2: Choose the option that tracks items or activity.

m0_s01_real_world_examples.hint: Think about systems with lots of records.

options.a: An online store tracking products and orders

m0_s01_select_all_true.title: Which foundation statements are true?
m0_s01_select_all_true.prompt: Select all true statements about SQL and databases.

help.concept: This combines the main ideas from the section.
help.hint_1: Match each choice to the core definitions.
help.hint_2: Ignore unrelated tools or wrong definitions.

m0_s01_select_all_true.hint: Match each statement to the core ideas.

options.a: SQL is used to work with stored data.
options.c: A row usually represents one record.
options.d: SQL is only for drawing user interfaces.
options.e: A database is the same thing as one single column.

m0_s01_reorder_sql_name.title: Build the full name of SQL
m0_s01_reorder_sql_name.prompt: Reorder the parts to form the full expanded name of SQL.
m0_s01_reorder_sql_name.concept: Put the three words of SQL in order.

m0_s01_reorder_select_meaning.title: What does this query mean?
m0_s01_reorder_select_meaning.prompt:
Put the ideas in order to match what this SQL query is saying.

~~~sql
SELECT * FROM students;
~~~
m0_s01_reorder_select_meaning.hint: Start with the action, then all data, then the table.
m0_s01_reorder_select_meaning.concept: This query reads data from the students table.

m0_s01_reorder_database_picture.title: Organize the big picture
m0_s01_reorder_database_picture.prompt: Reorder these from biggest container to smallest table idea.
m0_s01_reorder_database_picture.hint: A database holds tables. Tables contain rows and columns.
m0_s01_reorder_database_picture.concept: Think from the large data container to smaller table pieces.
m0_s01_reorder_database_picture.hint_2: Columns and rows belong inside a table.

m0_s01_reorder_sql_workflow.title: SQL learning flow
m0_s01_reorder_sql_workflow.prompt: Reorder the steps in the usual SQL workflow.
m0_s01_reorder_sql_workflow.hint: You ask first, then run, then read the result.
m0_s01_reorder_sql_workflow.concept: SQL usually follows a question, run, result cycle.
m0_s01_reorder_sql_workflow.hint_2: You see the result after running the query.

m0_s01_reorder_real_system.title: How a real system uses a database
m0_s01_reorder_real_system.prompt: Put these ideas in the most logical order.
m0_s01_reorder_real_system.hint: First information is stored, then organized, then queried.
m0_s01_reorder_real_system.concept: Real software stores data before asking SQL questions about it.
m0_s01_reorder_real_system.hint_1: Data must exist before you query it.
m0_s01_reorder_real_system.hint_2: Tables organize the information before SQL asks questions.

m0_s01_reorder_table_meaning.title: Understand a table
m0_s01_reorder_table_meaning.prompt: Reorder the statements so they explain a table clearly.
m0_s01_reorder_table_meaning.hint: Start with columns, then rows, then what a row represents.
m0_s01_reorder_table_meaning.concept: Columns describe data types, rows hold entries, and a row is usually one record.
m0_s01_reorder_table_meaning.hint_1: Columns explain what kind of data each part stores.
m0_s01_reorder_table_meaning.hint_2: A row is usually one full item.

tokens.t3: one row usually represents one record

m0_s01_fill_sql_full_name.title: Complete the SQL name
m0_s01_fill_sql_full_name.prompt: Fill in the blank with the correct word.

help.concept: SQL stands for Structured Query Language.
help.hint_1: The missing word is about asking for data.
help.hint_2: The full phrase is Structured Query Language.

m0_s01_fill_sql_full_name.hint: Think about the Q in SQL.

m0_s01_fill_database_contains.title: What can a database contain?
m0_s01_fill_database_contains.prompt: Choose the word that correctly completes the sentence.

help.concept: A database is the larger container.
help.hint_1: It can hold more than one of these.

m0_s01_fill_row_meaning.title: What does a row represent?
m0_s01_fill_row_meaning.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best answer.

help.concept: In a table, a row usually holds one full item or entry.
help.hint_1: Think about one student in a students table.
help.hint_2: A row usually means one full record.

m0_s01_fill_row_meaning.template: In a table, one row usually represents one ____.

m0_s01_fill_select_meaning.title: Meaning of SELECT
m0_s01_fill_select_meaning.prompt: Complete the sentence using the correct word.

help.concept: SELECT is used when you want to read or display data.
help.hint_1: It does not delete or rename anything.

m0_s01_fill_select_meaning.template: In SQL, SELECT usually means ____ data.

m0_s01_fill_sql_action.title: One common SQL action
m0_s01_fill_sql_action.prompt: Pick the word that makes the sentence true.

help.concept: SQL can read, sort, filter, and summarize data.
help.hint_2: You use this when you only want some rows.

m0_s01_fill_sql_action.template: SQL can help you ____ data to find only what you need.

m0_s01_fill_store_example.title: Real database example
m0_s01_fill_store_example.prompt: Complete the sentence with the best real-world answer.

help.concept: Online stores often save products, customers, and orders in a database.
help.hint_2: Stores often track products and orders.

m0_s01_fill_store_example.template: An online store database may track products and ____.
